title: Daily Market Digest: May 11, 2026
subtitle: Market sentiment cautiously bullish amid geopolitical tensions; major indices see record highs but face inflation concerns.

items.headline: US Futures Edge Lower as Geopolitical Tensions Reignite Oil Price Fears
items.summary: Monday, May 11, 2026, sees U.S. stock futures open cautiously, with oil prices surging over 4% after President Trump rejected Iran's latest peace proposal, amplifying concerns over prolonged disruption in the Strait of Hormuz and global energy supplies. This follows a week where major indices reached new record highs.

sections.title: Major Indices Performance (as of Friday, May 8, 2026)

items.headline: Lumentum Holdings ($LITE) Surges on Nasdaq-100 Inclusion
items.summary: Shares of Lumentum Holdings rose on Monday after Nasdaq Global Indexes announced the company would join the Nasdaq-100 Index.

items.headline: Cisco Systems ($CSCO) Continues Strong Performance
items.summary: Cisco Systems Inc. was a major gainer in the Dow Jones Industrial Average on Friday, May 8, surging 4.8%.

items.headline: DigitalOcean ($DOCN) Driven by AI Services Growth
items.summary: DigitalOcean's aggressive expansion into AI services has led to a significant year-to-date increase, with shares up 9.44% on Monday and 240% in 2026.

items.headline: Wendy's Company ($WEN) Drops on Downgrade
items.summary: The Wendy's Company shares fell on Monday after JPMorgan Chase & Co. downgraded the stock to Underweight, citing continued sales declines.

items.headline: Energy Sector ($XLE) Underperforms
items.summary: The Energy Select Sector SPDR fell 1.8% on Friday, May 8, as nine out of 11 S&P 500 sectors ended in negative territory.

items.headline: Kevin Warsh Expected to be Confirmed as Next Fed Chair This Week
items.summary: Jerome Powell's term as Federal Reserve Chair concludes this Friday, May 15, with Kevin Warsh anticipated to be confirmed as his successor by the US Senate this week. Warsh is perceived as more open to rate cuts, though a reduction is deemed unlikely without substantially softer inflation data. Goldman Sachs has delayed its forecast for Fed rate cuts to December 2026 and March 2027, citing persistent high energy prices and inflation.

items.headline: April CPI Release Set to Be This Week's Major Economic Catalyst
items.summary: Investors are keenly awaiting the release of April's Consumer Price Index (CPI) on Tuesday, May 12, at 8:30 AM ET, which is considered the single biggest market catalyst for the week. Consensus estimates project headline CPI to rise 0.6% month-over-month and 3.7% year-over-year, with core CPI expected at 0.3% MoM and 2.7% YoY. The University of Michigan's preliminary Consumer Sentiment Index for May registered 48.2%, falling below expectations and marking the lowest reading since June 2022.

items.headline: Semiconductors, Energy, Defense, and Industrials Lead in 2026; REITs, Discretionary, and Utilities Lag
items.summary: Year-to-date, sectors such as semiconductors, energy, defense, and industrials have demonstrated strong leadership in the S&P 500, with companies like Nvidia ($NVDA), Broadcom ($AVGO), Exxon Mobil ($XOM), Lockheed Martin ($LMT), and Caterpillar ($CAT) driving performance. Conversely, commercial REITs, consumer discretionary, and utilities have been lagging, often attributed to rate sensitivity and slower growth prospects. On Friday, nine of eleven S&P 500 sectors closed lower, including Energy, Industrials, Materials, and Utilities.

items.headline: Key Earnings Reports from Cisco, Alibaba, and Applied Materials Expected This Week
items.summary: Major companies reporting earnings this week include Cisco ($CSCO), Alibaba ($BABA), and Applied Materials ($AMAT). Cisco's stock surged 4.8% on Friday, May 8. Alibaba's earnings on Wednesday, May 13, are closely watched amid cautious expectations, while Applied Materials reports Thursday, May 14. Icon ($ICLR) also reported its Q1 2026 earnings today after market close.
